name: item-1
description: Plain-text formats are decoded directly from their bytes; while PDF documents are parsed with Apache Tika.

name: item-2
description: Virtual folders are expressed as slashes in the blob name; and recursion can be enabled per configured source.

name: item-3
description: An optional extension allowlist restricts which blobs in a folder listing are turned into documents.

name: item-4
description: Unsupported binaries such as images and Microsoft Office documents are skipped during folder loads.

name: item-5
description: When a blob is named explicitly; a deliberately unsupported type is reported as an error instead of skipped.

name: item-6
description: Listing follows the NextMarker pagination cursor so that large containers are fully enumerated.

name: item-7
description: Each returned document carries metadata: the file name; MIME type; size; and any available timestamps.

name: item-8
description: This fixture exists to exercise the loader end to end against a real storage account.

name: item-9
description: It contains several paragraphs so that extraction has meaningful content to return and assert on.

name: item-10
description: Text extraction should preserve the readable words on every page of a multi-page document.

name: item-11
description: The loader treats a path with a trailing slash as a folder prefix rather than an exact blob name.

name: item-12
description: A path without an extension that matches no blob is resolved as a folder prefix automatically.

name: item-13
description: Content is intentionally verbose to make the extracted output realistic rather than trivial.

name: item-14
description: Downstream retrieval-augmented generation pipelines consume these text documents as context.

name: item-15
description: The Azure Blob Storage data loader retrieves documents from one or more containers and returns them as text.
